Islam, Shadman BAN vs pace

Bangladesh M · LHB · How to exploit — pace plan
v1.4
22 Jul 2026
Runs
949
37 dismissals · 53 inns

3-yr 29.5

of team, per innings
3-yr 7.6%

15%
innings with ≥25% of team
3-yr 15%

Summary

  • A modest, steady batter — vulnerable to swing.
  • Plan for pace: bowl full outside off, gets it to seam away.
  • They get out most often to balls that are full and in the channel (3.7 dismissals/100).
  • Most often out caught behind (57% of dismissals); most often to right fast (26).

Common themes

  • left-hand bat, averaging 25.6 at a strike rate of 50.4.
  • Carries a big share of the batting — 11.4% of team runs.
  • Scores mainly through the leg side (49%).

Strengths

  • Main scoring shot is the work/nudge (36% of runs).
  • The cut brings them 16% of their runs vs pace — 2.1× the typical Test batter's share.
  • Starts securely — their dismissal rate barely drops once set (1.9 early vs 2.1/100); early pressure alone won't buy their wicket. Early runs come mainly off the work/nudge (40%).

Weaknesses / how to get him

  • Most often out caught behind (57% of dismissals); most often to right fast (26).
  • Vulnerable to the full (averages 20 there vs 26 overall; 3.1 dismissals/100).
  • Struggles outside off (18 avg, 2.2 outs/100).
  • The ball that seams away hurts them (avg 16 vs 39 when it holds its line).
  • Most dangerous ball: full and in the channel (3.7 dismissals/100, avg 16).
  • His pull/hook is high-risk (false shot 35%, 0.0 outs/100).

Match Impact (share of runs)

Makes 11.4% of their team's runs over a career (typical innings 7.6%), and 2.9% of all runs in their matches; carries the innings (≥25% of the team's runs) 15% of the time, and dominates (≥40%) 4%.

Suggested Fields (the stock field ± their evidenced deviations)

Each field starts from the stock template for that bowler type & phase (orthodox because it works), then makes at most three changes — each one earned by a trigger in their game clearing the cohort P75 bar (do they lap, cut, pull, score square, edge early?). Change rows are highlighted with their stat; Stock rows carry the orthodoxy line. The read line backtests the changes against the pure stock field. Early = first 30 balls; Set = once in. Drawn from behind the bowler (bowler bottom, striker top); off side is on the right.
Field vs right-arm pace
Early — first 30 balls · 770 balls
Stock field + 1 change (highlighted) — covering +6% of their boundary runs vs the pure stock field.

Floating fielder: They score only 4% of their runs where mid-off stands — the spare run-saver to move as the game asks.
Once set · 715 balls
Leg-side stock field — strong through the leg side (49% of their runs vs 24% off) — a squarer leg-side ring, one slip out. + 1 change (highlighted) — covering +6% of their boundary runs vs the pure stock field.

Bouncer plan
A standard bumper field — he isn't a heavy puller (P59), so this is a holding / surprise option, not a wicket-taking plan. One slip, a catcher in front of square, and the two legal leg-side riders (deep backward square + deep fine leg).
FielderStock/ChangeWhy they're there
Slip 1ChangeOne slip kept for the top / outside edge.
Short legChangeCatcher in front of square for the fend or glove off the hip.
GullyChangeThe steer or fend that flies squarer of the wicket.
PointChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
CoverChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Mid-offChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Mid-onChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Deep backward squareChangeDeep behind square for the top-edged pull.
Deep fine legChangeDeep behind square for the hook.
